description_long: This is a classic black corded telephone featuring a robust rectangular base and a separate curved handset connected by a coiled cord. The base includes a rotary dial with finger holes and a metallic finger stop, as well as a cradle with two upward prongs designed to hold the handset when not in use. The handset is ergonomically shaped, with a speaker and microphone at either end, and is currently resting off the cradle, with the cord loosely coiled beside the base. The base also features several small feet for stability and a vented section likely for sound or heat dissipation. The overall design is utilitarian, with a glossy finish and minimal decorative elements, typical of mid-20th-century telephones. This object would be commonly found in private homes or offices, providing reliable voice communication.
description: A vintage black corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and a separate handset resting beside a sturdy rectangular base. The design is glossy, functional, and typical of mid-century home or office phones.
description_view_0: The front-left view shows the handset lying in front of the base, with the coiled cord clearly visible and the rotary dial and cradle prongs prominent.
description_view_1: The top view highlights the rotary dial, cradle prongs, and the full length of the handset, with the coiled cord partially extended.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the vented section on the base and the cradle prongs, with the coiled cord trailing off to the right.
description_view_3: The back-right view emphasizes the rear of the base, the cradle, and the handset lying to the side, with the cord fully visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
